# Session 6 — 2026-03-18

## What I did
Investigated blocked Vercel deployments flagged by Carlos. Confirmed the latest deploy (dpl_RK8Q1CFXjHvrwHGZJmNwtM22V9m4, commit ca8d981) is READY and production returns HTTP 200. Identified root cause: sessions 4-5 pushed commits without triggering API gitSource deploys — production was stale across multiple sessions. Applied two systemic fixes: (1) MISTAKES.md Entry 005 — "Forgot to trigger API gitSource deploy after git push"; (2) genesis-prompt.md updated with mandatory post-deploy health check (poll READY + curl 200) and proactive session-start Vercel health check (step 7b).

## What worked
MCP Vercel tools (list_teams, list_projects, list_deployments) successfully retrieved deployment state after Vercel REST API v13 returned invalid API version errors. Pattern: use MCP tools for Vercel reads, Bash+curl for deploys.

## What didn't work / friction
The previously triggered deploy dpl_RK8Q1CFXjHvrwHGZJmNwtM22V9m4 returned "not found" when checked via REST API v13 — but MCP list_deployments confirmed it as READY. Version mismatch between REST API versions is a known issue; MCP tools are more reliable for reads.

## Manual actions this session
None — this was a pure protocol fix session.

## Pattern I'm noticing
Every time Carlos has to flag something operational (blocked deploys, wrong project, SSO), it means we had no proactive monitoring. The fix is always the same: add it to the session start sequence so it runs automatically. Session start now has 7b: proactive Vercel health check every run.

## Open questions
Waitlist signups still at 0 (no persistent DB). Distribution posts (Twitter, Reddit) went live session 5. Next session should check Carlos's email for admin signup notifications and update business_signals if any came in.
